objective: Stress-test every key dashboard claim: leave-one-out, winsorize, permutation, alternative specs

results.title: Permutation test: BB coverage → Friend trust (r=0.78)
results.description: Exact p-value from 1000 label-shuffling permutations
results.finding: r=0.7786, permutation p=0.0000 (1000 perms). Confirmed significant.

results.title: Permutation test: IDDE → Family trust (r=0.65)
results.description: Exact p-value from 1000 label-shuffling permutations
results.finding: r=0.6530, permutation p=0.0000 (1000 perms). Confirmed significant.

results.title: Permutation test: IDDE → Fraud (r=0.63)
results.description: Exact p-value from 1000 label-shuffling permutations
results.finding: r=0.6286, permutation p=0.0000 (1000 perms). Confirmed significant.

results.title: Holm correction: how many correlations survive?
results.description: Sequential Bonferroni (Holm) is stricter than BH. Compares to BH result (105/532).
results.finding: Nominal p<0.05: 166/532. After Holm correction: 20/532. After BH (from EXP-26): 105/532.
results.recommendation: If Holm is much stricter than BH, the dashboard should note which specific correlations are most robust.

results.title: Leave South out: BB coverage → Friend trust (r=0.78)
results.description: Correlation after removing all South states
results.finding: Full r=0.7786, without South r=0.6565, Δ=-0.1221.

results.title: Leave South out: IDDE → Family trust (r=0.65)
results.description: Correlation after removing all South states
results.finding: Full r=0.6530, without South r=0.5650, Δ=-0.0880.

results.title: Spearman vs Pearson: IDDE → Safety perception (R²=0.45)
results.description: Does rank correlation differ from Pearson? Tests for nonlinearity/outliers.
results.finding: Pearson r=0.2493, Spearman ρ=0.3738, Δ=+0.1245. Nonlinearity detected.

results.title: Panel year-by-year: IDDE → avg_wage
results.description: Does the cross-sectional relationship hold in each year separately?

results.title: Panel year-by-year: IDDE → crime_rate_100k
results.description: Does the cross-sectional relationship hold in each year separately?

results.title: R² stability (70% subsamples): IDDE → Family trust (r=0.65)
results.description: Distribution of R² across 500 random 70% subsets
results.finding: R² median=0.4327, IQR=0.1184. Unstable — sensitive to sample composition.

results.title: R² stability (70% subsamples): IDDE → Fraud (r=0.63)
results.description: Distribution of R² across 500 random 70% subsets
results.finding: R² median=0.4076, IQR=0.1269. Unstable — sensitive to sample composition.

results.title: LOO influence: BB coverage → Friend trust (r=0.78)
results.description: How much does removing one state change the correlation?
results.finding: Full r=0.7786. Max Δr=0.0283 (removing San Luis Potosí). Robust.
results.recommendation: If max_delta > 0.10, the correlation is driven by San Luis Potosí. Must disclose.

results.title: LOO influence: IDDE → Family trust (r=0.65)
results.description: How much does removing one state change the correlation?
results.finding: Full r=0.6530. Max Δr=0.0620 (removing Oaxaca). Robust.
results.recommendation: If max_delta > 0.10, the correlation is driven by Oaxaca. Must disclose.

results.title: LOO influence: IDDE → Fraud (r=0.63)
results.description: How much does removing one state change the correlation?
results.finding: Full r=0.6286. Max Δr=0.0757 (removing Ciudad de México). Robust.
results.recommendation: If max_delta > 0.10, the correlation is driven by Ciudad de México. Must disclose.

results.title: LOO influence: E-banking → Wage (R²=0.43)
results.description: How much does removing one state change the correlation?
results.finding: Full r=0.6525. Max Δr=0.0452 (removing Querétaro). Robust.
results.recommendation: If max_delta > 0.10, the correlation is driven by Querétaro. Must disclose.

results.title: LOO influence: IDDE → Safety perception (R²=0.45)
results.description: How much does removing one state change the correlation?
results.finding: Full r=0.2493. Max Δr=0.0857 (removing Ciudad de México). Robust.
results.recommendation: If max_delta > 0.10, the correlation is driven by Ciudad de México. Must disclose.

results.title: LOO influence: BB coverage → Wage (r=0.67)
results.description: How much does removing one state change the correlation?
results.finding: Full r=0.4750. Max Δr=0.0726 (removing San Luis Potosí). Robust.
results.recommendation: If max_delta > 0.10, the correlation is driven by San Luis Potosí. Must disclose.

results.title: Bootstrap 80% stability: BB coverage → Friend trust (r=0.78)
results.description: Correlation stability when using 80% subsamples (500 iterations)
results.finding: Bootstrap r: median=0.7804, 95% CI=[0.6976, 0.8372]. Range width=0.1396.

results.title: Bootstrap 80% stability: IDDE → Family trust (r=0.65)
results.description: Correlation stability when using 80% subsamples (500 iterations)
results.finding: Bootstrap r: median=0.6536, 95% CI=[0.5398, 0.7542]. Range width=0.2144.

results.title: Bootstrap 80% stability: IDDE → Fraud (r=0.63)
results.description: Correlation stability when using 80% subsamples (500 iterations)
results.finding: Bootstrap r: median=0.6331, 95% CI=[0.4793, 0.7361]. Range width=0.2568.

results.title: Winsorized (5%): BB coverage → Friend trust (r=0.78)
results.description: Correlation after clipping extreme values at 2.5%/97.5%
results.finding: Winsorized r=0.7945 vs original r=0.7786. Δ=0.0159. Robust to outliers.

results.title: Winsorized (5%): IDDE → Family trust (r=0.65)
results.description: Correlation after clipping extreme values at 2.5%/97.5%
results.finding: Winsorized r=0.6719 vs original r=0.6530. Δ=0.0190. Robust to outliers.

results.title: Winsorized (5%): IDDE → Fraud (r=0.63)
results.description: Correlation after clipping extreme values at 2.5%/97.5%
results.finding: Winsorized r=0.6028 vs original r=0.6286. Δ=0.0258. Robust to outliers.

results.title: Winsorized (5%): E-banking → Wage (R²=0.43)
results.description: Correlation after clipping extreme values at 2.5%/97.5%
results.finding: Winsorized r=0.6509 vs original r=0.6525. Δ=0.0016. Robust to outliers.

results.title: Winsorized (5%): IDDE → Safety perception (R²=0.45)
results.description: Correlation after clipping extreme values at 2.5%/97.5%
results.finding: Winsorized r=0.3019 vs original r=0.2493. Δ=0.0526. Sensitive to outliers.

results.title: Winsorized (5%): BB coverage → Wage (r=0.67)
results.description: Correlation after clipping extreme values at 2.5%/97.5%
results.finding: Winsorized r=0.4637 vs original r=0.4750. Δ=0.0113. Robust to outliers.

results.title: Spearman vs Pearson: BB coverage → Friend trust (r=0.78)
results.description: Does rank correlation differ from Pearson? Tests for nonlinearity/outliers.
results.finding: Pearson r=0.7786, Spearman ρ=0.7552, Δ=-0.0233. Consistent.

results.title: Spearman vs Pearson: IDDE → Family trust (r=0.65)
results.description: Does rank correlation differ from Pearson? Tests for nonlinearity/outliers.
results.finding: Pearson r=0.6530, Spearman ρ=0.6323, Δ=-0.0207. Consistent.

results.title: Spearman vs Pearson: IDDE → Fraud (r=0.63)
results.description: Does rank correlation differ from Pearson? Tests for nonlinearity/outliers.
results.finding: Pearson r=0.6286, Spearman ρ=0.5456, Δ=-0.0830. Consistent.

results.title: Spearman vs Pearson: E-banking → Wage (R²=0.43)
results.description: Does rank correlation differ from Pearson? Tests for nonlinearity/outliers.
results.finding: Pearson r=0.6525, Spearman ρ=0.6706, Δ=+0.0181. Consistent.

results.description: Does rank correlation differ from Pearson? Tests for nonlinearity/outliers.
results.finding: Pearson r=0.4750, Spearman ρ=0.4964, Δ=+0.0214. Consistent.

results.title: Trimmed means (20%): wage gap
results.description: Wage gap after trimming 20% from each tail to remove outlier influence
results.finding: Trimmed gap=$1360 vs raw gap=$1256. N(sustained)=3, N(inconsistent)=28.

results.title: R² stability (70% subsamples): BB coverage → Friend trust (r=0.78)
results.description: Distribution of R² across 500 random 70% subsets
results.finding: R² median=0.6132, IQR=0.0933. Stable.

results.title: Innovation subpillar → Crime rate
results.description: Which IDDE pillar drives the crime correlation?
results.finding: Innovation subpillar: r=0.2050, p=0.2687.
results.recommendation: If one pillar dominates, the dashboard should highlight that specific pillar.

results.title: Linear vs quadratic: BB coverage → Friend trust (r=0.78)
results.description: Does a quadratic term improve the fit?
results.finding: Linear R²=0.6062, Quadratic R²=0.6065, ΔR²=+0.0004. Linear is sufficient.

results.title: Linear vs quadratic: IDDE → Family trust (r=0.65)
results.description: Does a quadratic term improve the fit?
results.finding: Linear R²=0.4264, Quadratic R²=0.4271, ΔR²=+0.0007. Linear is sufficient.

results.title: Linear vs quadratic: IDDE → Fraud (r=0.63)
results.description: Does a quadratic term improve the fit?
results.finding: Linear R²=0.3951, Quadratic R²=0.4307, ΔR²=+0.0356. Linear is sufficient.
